content: Antibiotics are special medicines that help us fight harmful germs called bacteria when they make us sick. But sometimes, these bacteria learn how to protect themselves from the medicine. This is called antibiotic resistance. It happens when antibiotics are used too much or in the wrong way, like not finishing all the medicine or using it when it’s not needed. When bacteria become resistant, the medicine stops working, and it gets harder to make people better. These strong bacteria can spread from one person to another, making more people sick with germs that are hard to treat. To keep antibiotics working well, we should only use them when a doctor says it’s necessary and always finish our medicine. That way, we can help stop bacteria from becoming super strong and protect ourselves and others from getting really sick.
